questions.text: Choose the correct suffix to make a noun form of: 'durable'

Choose the word having nearest opposite meaning of the underlined word:
He is interested in the occult.

Choose the correct sentences:
(a) The table's leg was broken.
(b) The cover of the book was beautiful.
(c) The house's roof was painted.

Choose the correct sentences:
(a) I am knowing him for a long time.
(b) We have lived here for ten years.
(c) We haven't seen Padma for several months.

Which one of the following explains the meaning of the word 'Bibliophile'?
(a) One who is a great collector of stamps.
(b) One who is a great lover of books.
(c) One who is an expert in preparing bibliography.
(d) One who is a lover of birds and plants.

"Travelling by metro is better than travelling by bus."
If this sentence is changed into negative, the form of sentence will be:

options.A: Travelling by metro is not better than travelling by bus.
options.B: Travelling by bus is not better than travelling by metro.
options.C: Travelling by bus is not as good as travelling by metro.
options.D: Travelling by metro is not as good as travelling by bus.

questions.text:

You touched a _____ nerve when you mentioned his first wife.

Keep the news under your hat.

(b) There is no _____ for a disaster of this scale.

They rested at sunset.

Which of the following words explains the above meaning?
(a) illiterate  (b) inhumane  (c) countryside fellow  (d) ignoramus

'I still remember the joys of my childhood.'
Which one of the following is a correct complex sentence corresponding to the simple sentence above?
(a) I remember the joys experienced in my childhood.
(b) I still remember the joys which I experienced in my childhood.
(c) I still remember the joys I experienced in childhood.
(d) I still remember the joys which I had experienced in my childhood.

One should keep one's promises.

What were the effects of de-industrialization on India during British period?
(a) Burden on agriculture was decreased
(b) Independent rural economy was destroyed
(c) Decline of industrial centres
(d) Drive for economic Nationalism in India

(a) Salaries of Secretary State of India was from Indian expenditure.
(b) Public Service Commission was established through this Act.
(c) Importance of India Council was reduced.
(d) This act made provision for classification of the Central and State subjects.

(a) Establishment of Irrigation Commission
(b) Facilities of loan system
(c) To start 'Farmers Bank'
(d) To make it difficult for transfer of Farmers Land to moneylenders

(b) Pandit Jawaharlal Nehru National Park - (ii) Pench-Nagpur

Consider the following statements about the location of India.
(a) India has the central location between the east and west Asia.
(b) India's maritime contacts with the world have a longer history than its land based relationship.
(c) No other country has a long coastline on the Indian Ocean as India has.
(d) India shares its land with Pakistan and Afghanistan in the north-east.
Which of the above statements are correct?

(a) Deciduous Forests  (b) Broadleaf Forests
(c) Swamp Forests  (d) Thorn Forests

The wood of _____ tree is very soft so it is used to make the sticks of the match box.
The reform policies raised the foreign investment limit in banks upto _____ percent.

Statement (a): Indian Economy is primarily a rural economy.
Statement (b): As per, 2011 census the rural population of the country is about 83.25 crores (68.8% of the total population).

options.A: Statement (a) is true but (b) is false.
options.B: Statement (a) is false but (b) is true.
options.C: Both statement (a) and (b) are true and (b) is the correct explanation of (a).
options.D: Both statement (a) and (b) are true and (b) is the incorrect explanation of (a).

questions.text:
Since July 1991, the government has introduced a series of reforms in the trade sector. Among these the reform measures consisted of:
(a) Devaluation of the rupee
(b) Convertibility of rupee first on trade account and then on current account
(c) Liberalization of import, and decanalization of trade
(d) Measures to promote exports
Which of the following is correct?

Deepa Nayyar defined Globalisation as:
(a) Reduction in economic activities across political boundaries of nation.
(b) Expansion of economic activities across political boundaries of nation.
(c) The emergence of global markets for industrial sector only.
(d) The emergence of global market for agricultural and industrial market.
Who said that liberalization is going to promote economic growth and public welfare?
Growth in the index of wholesale prices is considered as an index of:
The government also liberalised the capital flows in form of _____.

(a) Macroeconomic stabilization
(b) Structural reforms
(c) National education policy

(a) There is a provision of independent Chairman for Standing Committee.
(b) Zilla Parishad Chairman is the Chairman of Standing Committee.
(c) Chief Executive Officer of Zilla Parishad is secretary of Standing Committee.
(d) Deputy Chief Executive Officer works is the secretary of Standing Committee of ZP.

(a) Appasaheb Dadagowda Patil  (b) Dattoba Pawar
(c) Tukaram Ganeshacharya  (d) Gangaram Kamble

options.D: all of the above are correct

questions.text:

(b) Scottish Mission started the first English school in Pune.
(c) The American Marathi Mission started the education of girls in Mumbai.

What is the current name given to the foreign policy implemented by India to increase relations with South-East Asian Countries?
Which rivers were given to India and Pakistan under the Indus water treaty between India and Pakistan in 1960?

options.A: Ravi, Sutlej and Beas to India and Indus, Chenab and Jhelum to Pakistan
options.B: Ravi, Sutlej and Beas to Pakistan and Indus, Chenab and Jhelum to India
options.C: Meghana, Padma, and Someshwari to India and Indus, Mahananda, and Kosi to Pakistan
